key_insights.insight: The interviewee experienced more peace after his arrest than he did during his corrupt tenure as a police officer.
key_insights.quote: It was the biggest moment of relief...This was the best feeling in the world, almost, like almost. I was like, finally it's over.

key_insights.insight: Systemic corruption can stem from perverse incentives, such as departments discouraging arrests due to budget constraints.
key_insights.quote: They'd rather have themselves crack than people getting robbed and raped and murdered. Does that make sense? Of course it does. Yeah, so it's all incentives.

key_insights.insight: When surrounded by corruption, ethical individuals can become part of the environment.
key_insights.quote: I became whatever was in the environment. I became part of the environment. I was no different than the people that were selling crack cocaine or robbing people, robbing drug dealers because they all did each other that way.

key_insights.insight: Police integrity training often focuses on consequences of corruption rather than the ethical implications and the destruction of public trust.
key_insights.quote: We weren't necessarily trained on integrity or ethics. We were trained on this is what would happen to you if. Don't take $5 from a motorist or $50 from a motorist because that will lead to one, you being arrested and being all over the news and then all the cops are going to hate you.

key_insights.insight: Living a double life creates significant anxiety and fear, particularly the fear of ruining your life.
key_insights.quote: When I was going to work every day, I was going to work with anxiety and fear...I was afraid of ruining my life...You know, everything's a lie. So there's anxiety and fear in that.

key_insights.insight: The need for love and approval, especially from a parent, can drive decision-making throughout life.
key_insights.quote: I've always been seeking my mother's approval for some reason...And that never has had left me.

key_insights.insight: It's easier to tell the truth than to lie, because you don't have to remember the lies.
key_insights.quote: It's easier to tell the truth in the end than it is to lie because you have to remember the lies every day and live with the pressure of being uncovered.

actionable_advice.advice: When facing a difficult situation, have the hard conversation now versus avoiding it.
actionable_advice.how_to: Address the issue head-on rather than letting it fester and become an even larger problem.
By accepting the consequences for the risk can lead to a better life.
By living an authentic life, it becomes easier to be a better person.

memorable_stories: Story of stealing porn collection from a dead guy.
Account of getting death threats and reporting a fellow officer.
Narrative of joining a plan to steal money but his partner was working as an informant.
contrarian_claims: Arrest was the biggest moment of relief, a moment of freedom.
Arguing that while he robbed drug dealers, he could be trusted by normal citizens for police service.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Corrupt Police Officer: I Arrested Drug Dealers, Then Sold Their Drugs! There’s Massive Corruption Going On Today! - Mike Dowd
